cta: Share this with your group chat of fellow beginners.
vo: It’s five o’clock, the fridge is a bit random, and nobody planned dinner. This is my favorite thing to hand to AI. I open a tool like ChatGPT or Claude and type: I have chicken, broccoli, rice, eggs and about thirty minutes to feed a family of four. Suggest three simple dinners, and mark the easiest one if I’m worn out. A few seconds later I’ve got three real options — and it flags the low-effort one for the nights I’m done. No new groceries. No overthinking. Just dinner, sorted.

beats.text: I have [chicken, broccoli, rice, eggs] and about [30 min] to feed [a family of 4]. Suggest 3 simple dinners — mark the easiest one if I’m worn out.

beats.answer:
1. Sheet-pan chicken + broccoli — 30 min, one tray.
2. Rice bowls — leftover veg, an egg on top.
3. Tomato pasta — pantry staples, done in 20.
Tip — start with #1 if you’re worn out.
